codeReview.summary: The reducer refactor removes the ref/effect auto-advance flow and keeps the hook API surface intact. I found no blocking regressions against the feature scope, but two exported setter wrappers subtly change functional-updater semantics.

issues.description: `setMappingSelection` computes functional updates from a closed-over value (`selection(mappingSelection)`) before dispatch. This differs from React's queued functional-setter semantics, so multiple functional updates in one tick can drop earlier updates.

`setNormalizationConfig` uses the same closed-over functional-updater pattern (`nextNormalizationConfig(normalizationConfig)`), which can stale-read when functional updates are queued before the next render.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature contract is internally inconsistent about loading/error helper names: expectedBehavior allows dispatcher-collapsed helpers, while verificationSteps requires zero matches for `startLoading|failLoading`.

summary: Reviewed feature m6-use-reducer-state-machine using the handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit diff for 7a9a365. Implementation meets the reducer-state-machine and auto-advance-ref removal goals with no blocking defects. Logged two non-blocking setter-semantic risks and one shared-state contract inconsistency.
